data.content: The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`), but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file into the intermediate output directory, but it doesn't add that file to the `Compile` item group. The target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`, which means it runs before compilation, but the compiler doesn't know about the generated file because it's not included in the `@(Compile)` items.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group using `<ItemGroup>` with `<Compile Include="..." />` inside the target, after generating the file.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

{"path": "/tmp/sv-f155861a774d473e8d157d3b27355732/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

toolArgs.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to the intermediate output directory but never adds it to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild generates the file, then the C# compiler runs without knowing it exists.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="...">` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```
